structured_output.summary: No display-regression or API-compatibility MUST_FIX found. The list-API change is additive and the fallback chain matches the decided priority (manual name → calendar title → meeting code). Two advisory gaps: calendar_title exists only in the lightweight summary payload, so any full-data (include=data) consumer of MeetingCard loses the calendar title; and prefilling the edit field with calendarTitle converts a no-op save into a persisted manual name that detaches from future calendar updates.

findings.title: calendar_title missing from full-data (include=data) responses
findings.evidence: meetings.py returns `(m.data or {}) if include_full_data else _meeting_list_data_summary(m.data)`; the raw data contains `calendar_event` but not `calendar_title`, while meeting-card.tsx reads only `meeting.data?.calendar_title`.
findings.recommendation: Add a client-side fallback such as `meeting.data?.calendar_title || meeting.data?.calendar_event?.title`, or confirm and document that the dashboard list never requests include=data.

findings.title: Edit prefill with calendarTitle can freeze the calendar title as a manual name
findings.evidence: `setEditedTitle(rawTitle || calendarTitle || "")` prefills the edit box; saving without changes persists the calendar title into data.name, which then always wins over future calendar-title updates.
findings.recommendation: Confirm this is intended; otherwise skip saving when the edited value equals calendarTitle and no manual name existed.

findings.title: Participants-derived title removal is an intentional display change
findings.evidence: Meetings with participants but no manual name or calendar title previously showed "{names}との会議" and will now show the meeting code (platform_specific_id).
findings.recommendation: Accept as planned (plan step 4 explicitly removes participant-title priority); mention it in release notes if users relied on the old label.

findings.title: Verify no remaining consumers of removed prop/variables
findings.evidence: The `participantsTitleTemplate` prop and local `participants` variable were removed from meeting-card.tsx; `copy.participantsMeetingTitle` may now be dead copy. Any other MeetingCard call site passing the prop would fail compilation.
findings.recommendation: Rely on `tsc --noEmit` from the verification contract and grep for `participantsTitleTemplate` / `participantsMeetingTitle` to remove dead copy strings.

local_verification: grep -rn 'include=data\|include="data"\|include: "data"' services/dashboard/src to confirm the list fetch never requests full data (validates F1)
grep -rn 'participantsTitleTemplate\|participantsMeetingTitle' services/dashboard/src to confirm no dead references remain (validates F4)
